It is generally considered that Letters of Credit offer a good balance of security between the buyer and the seller, because both the buyer and seller rely upon the security of banks and the banking system to ensure that payment is received and goods are provided. baseline: Joseph Smith, Jr. (December 23, 1805 – June 27, 1844) was an American prophet, seer, and revelator who founded the Latter Day Saint movement in 1831, and the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (LDS Church) in 1831 and 1836, respectively. He was also the first President of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (1846–1847) and the first and second President of the Nauvoo Legion (1841–1844). He was killed in Carthage, Illinois, on June 27, 1844, by a mob of anti-Mormon "Hawkeye" militia members. He was 38 years old. His death led to the exodus of the Latter Day Saint movement's membership from Nauvoo, Illinois, to the Salt Lake Valley, Utah, in 1846–1847. baseline: fascia (plural: fasciae) is a band or sheet of connective tissue that connects, supports, or surrounds muscles, bones, or organs. It is also known as a "bundle of sticks" due to its fibrous, fibrous-like structure. Fascia is composed of collagen and elastin, and it plays a crucial role in the body's ability to move and maintain posture. It is also involved in the transmission of force and the regulation of joint movement. Fascia can be found throughout the body, including the superficial fascia, deep fascia, and visceral fascia. The superficial fascia is located beneath the skin and connects to underlying muscles, while the deep fascia is found between muscles and bones. Visceral fascia surrounds organs and helps to maintain their position and function. Fascia is also involved in the body's ability to adapt to different physical demands and can be affected by various factors, such as injury, aging, and disease. It is an important area of study in the field of myofascial release, which aims to release tension and improve mobility by manipulating the fascia. Overall, fascia is a vital component of the body's musculoskeletal system and plays a crucial role in maintaining overall health and function.

It also limited Germany's military and prohibited the union of Germany and Austria. The clause was intended to weaken Germany's ability to wage war and prevent future conflicts. However, it was later criticized for being too harsh and contributing to the rise of Adolf Hitler and the outbreak of World War II. The clause was officially removed from the treaty in 1951.

baseline: Article II, Section 1, Clause 2 of the United States Constitution. This clause establishes the process for electing the President and Vice President of the United States. It also provides for the Electoral College, a body of electors that indirectly elect the President and Vice President. In classical Greece, her abduction by Paris—or elopement with him—was a popular motif. In medieval illustrations, this event was frequently portrayed as a seduction, whereas in Renaissance painting it is usually depicted as a rape by Paris. Interchangeable usage of the terms rape and elopement often lends ambiguity to the legend.[][1] Inspired by the line, "Was this the face that launched a thousand ships...?" from Marlowe's Faustus, Isaac Asimov jocularly coined the unit "millihelen" to mean the amount of beauty that can launch one ship.
